**Brunswick** ([/ˈbrʌnzwɪk/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*BRUN-zwik*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key") or [/ˈbrʌnswɪk/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*BRUN-swik*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key")) is the largest city in [Medina County, Ohio](/wiki/Medina_County,_Ohio "Medina County, Ohio"), United States. The population was 35,426 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[4]](#cite_note-2020_Census_(City)-4) It is approximately 20 miles (32 km) southwest of [Cleveland](/wiki/Cleveland "Cleveland") and part of the [Cleveland metropolitan area](/wiki/Greater_Cleveland "Greater Cleveland").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Brunswick was founded on January 1, 1815, and was named randomly in a naming contest after [Brunswick](/wiki/Braunschweig "Braunschweig"), Germany.[[6]](#cite_note-6) It was incorporated as a village on February 1, 1960, and incorporated as a city on October 2, 1960.[[7]](#cite_note-co.medina.oh.us-7)

On June 23, 2014 a tornado hit Brunswick, and the tornado was rated a high-end EF1 or EF2.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Brunswick is located at [41°14′47.17″N 81°49′11.15″W﻿ / ﻿41.2464361°N 81.8197639°W﻿ / 41.2464361; -81.8197639](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Brunswick,_Ohio&params=41_14_47.17_N_81_49_11.15_W_type:city) (41.2464362, -81.8197626),[[5]](#cite_note-gnis-5)

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the city has a total area of 13.031 square miles (33.75 km2), of which, 12.995 square miles (33.66 km2) is land and 0.036 square miles (0.09 km2) is water.[[2]](#cite_note-CenPopGazetteer2024-2)

### 2020 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2020 census")]

As of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), there were 35,426 people, 14,187 households, and 9,702 families residing in the city. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 2,733.5 inhabitants per square mile (1,055.4/km2), and there were 14,714 housing units at an average density of 1,135.3 per square mile (438.3/km2).[[12]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-12)

The median age was 41.5 years; 21.4% of residents were under the age of 18 and 18.0% were 65 years of age or older. For every 100 females there were 95.3 males, and for every 100 females age 18 and over there were 93.9 males age 18 and over.[[12]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-12)

There were 14,187 households in Brunswick, of which 29.5% had children under the age of 18 living in them. Of all households, 52.7% were married-couple households, 16.4% were households with a male householder and no spouse or partner present, and 24.5% were households with a female householder and no spouse or partner present. About 26.5% of all households were made up of individuals and 11.4% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older.[[12]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-12)

There were 14,714 housing units, of which 3.6% were vacant. Among occupied housing units, 76.3% were owner-occupied and 23.7% were renter-occupied. The homeowner vacancy rate was 0.6% and the rental vacancy rate was 6.8%.[[12]](#cite_note-Census2020DP-12)

100.0% of residents lived in urban areas, while 0% lived in rural areas.[[13]](#cite_note-Census2020DHC-13)

### American Community Survey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: American Community Survey")]

As of the 2023 [American Community Survey](/wiki/American_Community_Survey "American Community Survey"), there are 14,482 estimated households in Brunswick with an average of 2.42 persons per household. The city has a median household income of $85,313. Approximately 6.1% of the city's population lives at or below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_in_the_United_States "Poverty in the United States"). Brunswick has an estimated 68.8% employment rate, with 28.7% of the population holding a bachelor's degree or higher and 93.8% holding a high school diploma.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

The top five reported ancestries (people were allowed to report up to two ancestries, thus the figures will generally add to more than 100%) were English (95.7%), Spanish (1.1%), Indo-European (2.6%), Asian and Pacific Islander (0.2%), and Other (0.4%).

### 2010 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2010 census")]

As of the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") there were 34,255 people, 12,967 households, and 9,565 families living in the city. The population density was 2,652.2 inhabitants per square mile (1,024.0/km2). There were 13,600 housing units at an average density of 1,052.6 per square mile (406.4/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 95.48% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._census) "White (U.S. census)"), 1.23% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.15% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 1.23% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.02% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 0.60% from some other races and 1.30% from two or more races. [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") people of any race were 2.31% of the population.

Of the 12,967 households 36.2% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 58.3% were married couples living together, 10.6% had a female householder with no husband present, 4.9% had a male householder with no wife present, and 26.2% were non-families. 21.9% of households were one person and 7.5% were one person aged 65 or older. The average household size was 2.63 and the average family size was 3.07.

The median age was 39.1 years. 25.2% of residents were under the age of 18; 7.5% were between the ages of 18 and 24; 27% were from 25 to 44; 28.3% were from 45 to 64; and 11.9% were 65 or older. The gender makeup of the city was 49.1% male and 50.9% female.

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the [2000 census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census") there were 33,388 people, 11,883 households, and 9,280 families living in the city. The population density was 2,662.3 inhabitants per square mile (1,027.9/km2). There were 12,251 housing units at an average density of 976.9 per square mile (377.2/km2). The racial makeup of the city was 97.09% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._census) "White (U.S. census)"), 0.74% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.13% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 0.86% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.02% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 0.38% from some other races and 0.78% from two or more races. [Hispanic or Latino](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans") people of any race were 1.36% of the population.

Of the 11,883 households 39.3% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 65.3% were married couples living together, 9.3% had a female householder with no husband present, and 21.9% were non-families. 17.7% of households were one person and 5.2% were one person aged 65 or older. The average household size was 2.79 and the average family size was 3.18.

The age distribution was 27.7% under the age of 18, 8.1% from 18 to 24, 32.7% from 25 to 44, 23.2% from 45 to 64, and 8.2% 65 or older. The median age was 35 years. For every 100 females, there were 96.5 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 94.4 males.

The median household income was $56,288 and the median family income was $62,080. Males had a median income of $42,675 versus $27,882 for females. The per capita income for the city was $21,937. About 3.2% of families and 4.6% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_threshold "Poverty threshold"), including 5.7% of those under age 18 and 5.0% of those age 65 or over.

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Education")]

Public education in Brunswick is administered by the [Brunswick City School District](/wiki/Brunswick_City_School_District "Brunswick City School District"), which operates seven elementary schools, one middle school, and [Brunswick High School](/wiki/Brunswick_High_School_(Ohio) "Brunswick High School (Ohio)"). Other schools in the city include St. Ambrose School, a [Roman Catholic](/wiki/Catholic "Catholic") [parochial school](/wiki/Parochial_school "Parochial school") serving Kindergarten through 8th grade.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

Brunswick University Center opened in 2011 as an extension of [Cuyahoga Community College](/wiki/Cuyahoga_Community_College "Cuyahoga Community College")’s Western Campus in [Parma](/wiki/Parma,_Ohio "Parma, Ohio").[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Brunswick has a public library, a branch of Medina County District Library.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Media")]

Brunswick is served by a daily newspaper, *The Medina County Gazette*[[20]](#cite_note-20) which is published every day of the week except Sundays and a free weekly newspaper, *The Brunswick Post*[[21]](#cite_note-21) which is published every Saturday. In addition, the *[Akron Beacon Journal](/wiki/Akron_Beacon_Journal "Akron Beacon Journal")* and the *[Cleveland Plain Dealer](/wiki/Cleveland_Plain_Dealer "Cleveland Plain Dealer")* occasionally cover the city and Medina County. Brunswick is served by numerous television and radio stations from both the [Greater Cleveland](/wiki/Greater_Cleveland "Greater Cleveland"), [Greater Akron](/wiki/Akron_metropolitan_area "Akron metropolitan area") and [Greater Canton](/wiki/Canton%E2%80%93Massillon,_Ohio_metropolitan_area "Canton–Massillon, Ohio metropolitan area") areas.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Brunswick,_Ohio&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Dean Heil](/wiki/Dean_Heil "Dean Heil"), former [folkstyle](/wiki/Collegiate_wrestling "Collegiate wrestling") [wrestler](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling"), two-time [NCAA wrestling](/wiki/NCAA_Division_I_Wrestling_Championships "NCAA Division I Wrestling Championships") champion at [Oklahoma State](/wiki/Oklahoma_State_Cowboys_wrestling "Oklahoma State Cowboys wrestling")
* [Pete Kostelnick](/wiki/Pete_Kostelnick "Pete Kostelnick"), ultramarathon runner
* [Natalie Sideserf](/wiki/Natalie_Sideserf "Natalie Sideserf"), cake artist, star of Texas Cake House
* [Ricky Wysocki](/wiki/Ricky_Wysocki "Ricky Wysocki"), professional disc golfer
